title: Senior Data Engineer
description_text:
We are looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong expertise in designing, building, and maintaining scalable data platforms. The ideal candidate should have hands-on experience in data modeling and developing end-to-end ETL pipelines using AWS services. You will work extensively with AWS Glue and AWS DMS for data integration and migration, and manage data stored in Snowflake, Oracle, and AWS RDS.
The role requires strong programming skills in Python and PySpark to process large-scale datasets efficiently. Candidates should be proficient in SQL for data analysis, transformations, and performance tuning. Experience with DBT for data transformations and maintaining analytics-ready datasets is highly desirable.
You should have exposure to CI/CD pipelines, GitLab, and version control best practices to support automated deployments and collaborative development. The role involves working closely with cross-functional teams to ensure data quality, optimize performance, and deliver reliable data solutions aligned with business requirements. Strong problem-solving skills and experience in cloud-based data engineering environments are essential.
Data Modelling, Glue, DMS, Snowflake, Oracle, Python, PySpark, CI/CD, DBT, Gitlab, AWS RDS, ETL Pipeline, SQL
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves designing, building, and maintaining scalable data platforms, as well as developing end-to-end ETL pipelines. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure data quality and deliver reliable data solutions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have strong expertise in data modeling and hands-on experience with AWS services. Proficiency in Python, PySpark, and SQL is required, along with experience in CI/CD pipelines and DBT.

linkedin_org_description:
We are a team of highly experienced sustainability experts, bringing over 20 years of wide-ranging technical, sectoral, and cultural expertise to your projects in Central Asia. 
This includes providing services around renewable energy supply, energy efficiency, ‘green’ technology and finance, climate change mitigation, water sustainability, green tourism, nature-based solutions, and sustainable consumption and production (SCP). This makes Unison one of the leading organisations contributing to the circular economy in the Central Asia region. We collaborate with clients to sustainably support their efforts in innovating and achieving long-term performance improvements.
We advise governments, financial institutions, donors and civil society organizations on the most critical issues and provide sustainable solutions for Central Asia. 
Our partners include renowned international donor organisations and finance institutions, nonprofit organisations, and state authorities at the national, regional and local levels. 
We aim for large-scale, long-term positive changes in the energy, climate, government and environmental sectors.

title: Service Driver
description_text:
Initial Washroom Hygiene Service Driver
Join Our Team and Make a Difference!
We are currently seeking a Service Driver to join our dedicated team at the Fareham branch, covering the Southampton area. If you enjoy driving, managing your own schedule and providing excellent customer service, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!
Why Join Rentokil Initial?
Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £25732 per annum.
Expected OTE: £26500 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available.
Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards.
Relocation Package: Moving from more than 2 hours away? We may offer up to £5,000 to help you settle in.
Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary.
Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers’ needs.
The Service Driver Role
As a driver, you will visit a number of different customer sites to service their washroom products. These products may include feminine hygiene units, nappy bins, air fresheners, soap dispensers and floor mats. Key responsibilities include: 
Loading and unloading units and floor mats for customers, so you will need to be physically fit for this role
Ensuring that your vehicle is safe at all times, driving responsibly and following our RI Drive Smart guidance
Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times
Service Driver Requirements:
Full UK driving licence held for more than two years, with no more than six penalty points
Demonstrate excellent customer service and communication skills
Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs
Ability to use own initiative and have a positive ‘can do’ attitude
Pride in the job you do
You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for
Service Driver Benefits:
Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes.
Access to a company vehicle and fuel card.
Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business.
Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme.
Enrolment in our company pension scheme.
Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards.
Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers.
Leads Commission scheme available - although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads.
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends.
Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service.
Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial
A Company Putting “People First”
Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world’s leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world’s leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world’s leading provider of plants and scenting.
As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.
Our Social Links
Website
LinkedIn
Facebook
Instagram


Rentokil Initial are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data view our careers privacy policy here

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must hold a full UK driving licence for over two years with minimal penalty points. Excellent customer service skills and a positive attitude are essential, along with the ability to work flexibly.

title: 3883-System Engineer-II (Citrix)
description_text:
About the Role
As an L2/L3 engineer, you will manage and optimize a high-performance hybrid infrastructure. You will bridge the gap between Citrix XenApp/XenDesktop environments and modern cloud ecosystems like AWS (EC2, S3) and Azure. By leveraging Python, PowerShell, and Bash, you will automate complex workflows, ensuring our network is secure, scalable, and resilient.
What You Need
Optimize Cloud Performance: Manage AWS EC2 instances and S3 buckets alongside Azure resources to ensure high availability.
Master Citrix & Networking: Administer XenApp/XenDesktop 7.x and NetScaler appliances (load balancing, gateway, policies).
Automate Everything: Use Python, PowerShell, and Bash to reduce manual overhead and drive operational excellence.
Lead Support: Act as the L2/L3 escalation point for complex Citrix, network, and Windows server issues.
What You Will Do
● Experience: 5+ years in IT infrastructure.
● Tech Stack: Citrix Virtual Apps/Desktops, NetScaler, Windows Server, Active Directory, and DNS/DHCP.
● Cloud & Virtualization: Hands-on experience with AWS/Azure and VMware/Hyper-V.
● Scripting: Proficiency in PowerShell, Python, or Bash.
● Communication: Excellent verbal and written skills for a collaborative 24/7 environment
We offer competitive benefits to set you up for success in and outside of work.
Here’s What We Offer
Generous Leaves: Enjoy generous leave benefits of up to 40 days.
Parental Leave: Leverage one of industry's best parental leave policies to spend time with your new addition.
Sabbatical: Want to focus on skill development, pursue an academic career, or just take a break? We've got you covered.
Health Insurance: We offer comprehensive health insurance to support you and your family, covering medical expenses related to illness, disease, or injury. Extending support to the family members who matter most.
Care Program: Whether it’s a celebration or a time of need, we’ve got you covered with care vouchers to mark major life events. Through our Care Vouchers program, employees receive thoughtful gestures for significant personal milestones and moments of need.
Financial Assistance: Life happens, and when it does, we’re here to help. Our financial assistance policy offers support through salary advances and personal loans for genuine personal needs, ensuring help is there when you need it most.
Innovaccer is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where all employees, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, marital status, or veteran status, feel valued and empowered.
Disclaimer: Innovaccer does not charge fees or require payment from individuals or agencies for securing employment with us. We do not guarantee job spots or engage in any financial transactions related to employment. If you encounter any posts or requests asking for payment or personal information, we strongly advise you to report them immediately to our HR department at px@innovaccer.com. Additionally, please exercise caution and verify the authenticity of any requests before disclosing personal and confidential information, including bank account details.
About Innovaccer
Innovaccer activates the flow of healthcare data, empowering providers, payers, and government organizations to deliver intelligent and connected experiences that advance health outcomes. The Healthcare Intelligence Cloud equips every stakeholder in the patient journey to turn fragmented data into proactive, coordinated actions that elevate the quality of care and drive operational performance. Leading healthcare organizations like CommonSpirit Health, Atlantic Health, and Banner Health trust Innovaccer to integrate a system of intelligence into their existing infrastructure, extending the human touch in healthcare. For more information, visit www.innovaccer.com.
Check us out on YouTube, Glassdoor, LinkedIn, Instagram, and the Web.

title: Storyly - Sales Development Representative
description_text:
Lead scalable outreach and experimentation with a global growth team.
Full-time | Fully Remote
We're Storyly, and we exist to help brands create engaging, interactive experiences for their customers. Our platform enables businesses to build Stories, just like the ones you see on Instagram, directly into their mobile apps and websites. 
We're a fast-moving team that values experimentation, smart use of technology, and getting things done. We believe in empowering our people to test new ideas, use the best tools available, and constantly improve how we work. If you're someone who gets excited about automation, scale, and finding creative ways to connect with people, you'll fit right in here.
In simple terms, we help apps show the right content to the right user at the right moment, visually, intuitively, and at scale.
We work with ambitious product and growth teams that care deeply about experimentation, performance, and clarity of communication. 
At Storyly, we value growth mindset, integrity, curiosity, and craftsmanship. We’re a tight-knit, international team that loves tools, learning, and building things that actually get used.
We value people who are honest, collaborative, fun to work with, and excited to build something meaningful without losing sight of quality, consistency, and long-term impact.
We're looking for a Sales Development Representative who's excited about using automation, smart workflows, and creative outreach to connect with enterprise clients at scale. You should be someone who's curious about new tools, loves experimenting with technology, and knows how to balance speed with quality.
In this role, you will:
🚀 Build, run, and improve high-scale enterprise outreach campaigns using the existing go-to-market strategy 
🤝 Collaborate closely with Growth, Content, and Product teams to understand ICPs and craft tailored value propositions 
🔧 Proactively test and introduce new outreach tools, automations, formats, and workflows to improve results 
✍️ Create and customize outbound content and CTAs that resonate with target ICPs and industries 
📊 Use technology, integrations, and automation platforms to execute account-based marketing outreach with precision and speed 
Working Hours: This is a full-time, fully remote role, and candidates must be available to work fully within GMT hours.
To excel in this role, you need:
✅ 2+ years of experience in a B2B SDR/BDR role 
✅ Strong command of outbound tools such as Apollo, Outreach, ZoomInfo, or similar platforms 
✅ Hands-on experience with automation platforms like n8n, Zapier, or similar 
✅ Ability to create and customize outbound content and CTAs based on target ICPs and industry 
✅ Strong command of English with excellent written and spoken communication skills 
Brownie points:
⭐ Ability to create repeatable workflows across multiple platforms and tools 
⭐ Ability to use GenAI models for audio, quick videos, gifs, or images 
⭐ Familiarity with designing and carrying out experimentation-driven outbound marketing (creative testing, iterations) 
⭐ Prior exposure to fast-moving startup or scale-up environments, especially in B2B, ecommerce, shopping, or retail verticals 
Our ideal profile includes being curious and fast-learning, with a track record of experimenting with business development methods that leverage automations, LLMs, and other high-scale approaches. You're proactive, self-driven, and comfortable with ambiguity. 
You thrive in collaborative environments, welcome feedback, and balance attention to detail with a pragmatic approach to execution. You're excited by experimentation, new tools, and innovative ways of working, and you solve problems with a constructive, solution-oriented mindset.
What’s in it for you?
💰 Competitive salary pack+ bonus/ commision scheme *
🌍 Full-time, fully remote role
🏖️ 15 days of paid time off
🧠 A culture that values learning, experimentation, and growth
🤝 A collaborative, international team that’s genuinely fun to work with
*Please note that this is a gross salary and that you are responsible for any tax-related procedures in your country of residence. We kindly ask you to apply only if you are happy to work remotely as an independent contractor.
This is our typical hiring process - occasionally, it may include extra steps.
For this role, JobRack is partnering with Storyly to find their next hire. We’ll handle all the screening and testing, so they see only the best-fit candidates - like you!
🔍 How to shine
• Read the role details carefully.
• Submit a thoughtful, high-quality application.
• Stay responsive - we’ll keep you updated at every stage.
✨ Important notes
• Apply only through JobRack; please don’t contact the company directly.
• We expect a respectful hiring experience - any disrespect means instant disqualification.
• This is a full-time, primary role (no freelancing or second jobs).
❓ New to remote work?
Check out our handy FAQ: jobrack.eu/jobseeker-faq

You will also proactively test and introduce new outreach tools and create customized outbound content.

title: Senior Quant Researcher (Prop / Systematic trading)
description_text:
We are a prop-trading firm that blends the agility of a startup with the capabilities of a high-performing fund. We build advanced, data-driven trading strategies across asset classes, and foster a culture where ideas matter, ownership is encouraged, and every team member can unlock their full potential.
This role is for a Senior quant who wants to own the full lifecycle of a strategy: generate ideas, research and validate them properly, deploy to production, and run the strategy with robust risk control. 
We can provide a strategy direction / starting thesis and the data/infra — you take it to a live, trading system.
We care about good decisions and robust strategies, not about specific models or fashionable architectures.ML is a tool, not a goal.
What you will do
Formulate and test investment hypotheses; turn them into live systematic strategies.
Build the full pipeline: data, signals/features, model, execution, risk, monitoring.
Design validation for markets: non-IID data, regime shifts, leakage control, walk-forward.
Own risk: position sizing, limits, stress tests, scenario analysis, tail-risk awareness.
Model real trading constraints: transaction costs, slippage, market impact, and capacity.
Run post-trade analytics: PnL attribution, drawdown analysis, signal decay, and model drift.
Partner with research/engineering/execution to make sure strategies actually trade.
What success looks like
You can take a thesis from “idea” to a live strategy with solid validation and risk controls.
You can explain what drives PnL, when it breaks, and how you detect/mitigate drift.
You raise the bar for research rigor and production readiness.
5+ years in quantitative research, systematic trading, or ML-driven modeling for markets.
Proven production deployment of strategies/models with measurable outcomes (live trading or equivalent).
Strong stats + time-series fundamentals (non-stationarity, dependence, tails, robustness).
Strong Python and engineering discipline (reproducible research, clean code, tests/monitoring mindset).
PM-style thinking: returns vs risk vs costs, not “models for models”.
English and Russian are both working languages, and we are particularly open to Russian-speaking quants with international backgrounds. We work in an international, multilingual team. 
Nice to have
Experience trading options/volatility, strong ML for time series, or a deep applied math/physics/econophysics background is a major plus.
Tech (what you’ll use)
Python; PyTorch if ML-heavy
Docker; experiment/research reproducibility tools (DVC/MLflow—tooling not strict)
Large, multi-source datasets (market + macro + alternative data)
You are not expected to be an expert in every area.We value ownership, depth, and good judgment over trying to cover everything at once.
Culture of innovation — a genuinely open, research-driven environment where curiosity is rewarded and your ideas directly shape real trading strategies.
True flexibility — work from anywhere; we care about outcomes, not where or when you sit at your desk.
High autonomy & ownership — no micromanagement, no bureaucracy. You get full responsibility over your research direction, models, and production impact.
Startup agility, Fund resources — fast decision-making, minimal red tape, and access to the data, compute, and infrastructure you need to run serious research.
Massive data advantage — work with a uniquely rich multi-modal dataset (order log, options chains, satellite data, alt-data, Bloomberg, proprietary feeds).
Top-tier equipment — choose the hardware/software setup that makes you most productive.
Well-being support — 35 days of vacation, 100% paid sick leave, and access to a corporate psychologist.
Real career growth — shape research culture, lead initiatives, and influence long-term strategy directions.
ai_core_responsibilities: Formulate and test investment hypotheses, turning them into live systematic strategies while managing the full pipeline from data to execution. Own risk management aspects including position sizing, limits, and stress tests.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of experience in quantitative research or systematic trading, with proven production deployment of strategies. Strong skills in statistics, Python, and a focus on risk versus returns are essential.

title: Senior Technical Artist
description_text:
🚀 Univrse is a Barcelona-based content creation VR studio developing premium multi-user immersive and interactive XR experiences. Our multidisciplinary and international team combines the physical, digital, and virtual worlds to create fascinating experiences.❤️ We love to open new paths to find unconventional solutions in the field of XR technologies.

We are now looking for a Senior Technical Artist who will play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between art and technology. You’ll work closely with artists and developers to ensure that our creative visions are translated into innovative, high-performance experiences. This is a full-time, on-site role, requiring your presence in the office for optimal collaboration and creativity. The role reports directly to the Head of Art, supporting the team in creating high-quality, innovative VR experiences and adaptations, with a focus on both artistic and technical solutions.
Key Responsibilities:
Provide technical follow-up to the art team, ensuring optimal performance and adherence to established guidelines.
Design and develop new art pipeline tools with a focus on usability and efficiency, enforcing best practices and coding standards within the team.
Analyze the creative process to identify and implement opportunities for enhancing tools and optimizing workflows.
Initiate and lead research and development efforts, contributing to technical pipeline development.
Create and maintain comprehensive technical documentation to support tools, workflows, and processes.
Act as the primary liaison between the art and programming teams to ensure seamless collaboration.
Mentor junior and intermediate team members, fostering their growth and professional development.
Specialize in the use of XRoam (Univrse internal technology platform) to create immersive experiences and adapt them to meet project needs.
Support content production art teams in achieving top-quality results.
Develop technical tools and solutions to solve performance issues while ensuring the artistic and gameplay vision is executed.
Identify and develop art-pipeline optimizations and tools for new and existing production workflows.
Optimize memory and performance while respecting project constraints.
Implement technical art features with the help of the programming team.
Cooperate with other programmers, contributing to a multidisciplinary team.
Offer technical support to the art team on day-to-day production issues.
Create and maintain detailed technical documentation.
Share best practices and new tools with other artists and programmers.
Act as a bridge between artistic complexities and development, collaborating closely with the Head of Art to ensure smooth integration of both areas.
Required Qualifications:
5+ years of experience in high-level coding languages (Python, C#).
Extensive hands-on experience with Unity Engine and strong proficiency in its application for developing high-quality experiences, particularly for standalone virtual reality projects.
Experience in shader development, including HLSL, and a solid understanding of the URP pipeline for VR Mobile applications.
Solid understanding of code architecture to develop maintainable tools, streamline workflows, and optimize pipeline efficiency.
Experience with Blender, Houdini, and Painter.
Solid knowledge of the digital art content creation pipeline, including high and low-poly modeling, unwrapping, baking, texturing, and level building.
A collaborative team-oriented mindset, with a strong desire to participate in a team-based pipeline across multiple locations.
Experience in real-world VR projects, specifically standalone VR.
Passionate about fun and constantly looking for opportunities to learn and improve.
Open to new ideas and perspectives, and willing to collaborate with diverse teams to build the future of entertainment.
Preferred Qualifications:
Significant experience in the video game/interactive entertainment industry, with expertise in a digital art content creation pipeline.
Strong experience with Unity 3D and understanding of the strengths and limitations of mobile platforms.
Proven experience working on standalone VR projects and a solid understanding of VR-specific design and performance optimization.
Proficiency in using Unity3D, C#, or C++.
Proven experience in a full digital art content creation pipeline, including modeling, unwrapping, baking, texturing, and level art.
Solid scripting skills (C#, Python).
Knowledge and interest in the following tech-art areas: Shading, VFX/Particles, Scripting, Lighting, Rendering and Post-Effects, SFX, Rigging, Physics.
Strong communication skills with the ability to liaise between technical and non-technical teams.
Attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to multitask and adapt to change.
Fluent in English.
Nice to have: 
Experience with profiling tools.
Experience with game editors, engines, and multiple art and animation pipelines.
Background in various tech-art areas.
Familiarity with the latest advancements in procedural content creation.
Knowledge of mathematics, including trigonometry and linear algebra, and the ability to apply these concepts effectively.
Multiple shipped projects as a Technical Artist.
Training and mentoring skills are a plus.
Knowledge of Spanish or Catalan is a plus.
🚀 What do we offer?

💫 A young, international, and dynamic work environment that is constantly growing.
😎 The chance to work on cutting-edge experiences.
🎯 Work with talented people who are industry pioneers.
🤓 Opportunities to experiment with innovative technologies.
✅ A competitive salary based on knowledge and experience.
🍌🍏 Breakfast, fruit & snacks.
🏃🏽‍♀️ Beach volleyball on Tuesdays (during the summer).
🌱 Creative retreat with the whole team.
👌🏽 Frequent cultural and leisure activities.

title: Senior Finance Business Partner (Operations & Commercial Marketing)
description_text:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is what happens when history, purpose and daring talent come together. Backed by TWG Global and GM, our team is uniquely positioned to disrupt Formula 1®, bringing a fresh perspective and an unrelenting drive for success. We have the energy of a start-up, with the ideas and originality of a business that always wants to lead, never wants to follow. 
We’re building everything from the ground up, from a high-performance car to an inclusive, values-driven culture. We show bold ambition. We combine leadership in innovation with excellence in execution. We are one team. We have the freedom to think differently, the opportunity to shape process and practice, an ego-free environment where people thrive on being challenged by those around them. A historic name behind us. Career-defining moments ahead. 

A New Chapter Begins.
Fueled by bold ambition
Play your part in getting us on the grid.
Closing Date: 13th February 2026
We are seeking a Senior Finance Business Partner to provide financial leadership to our Operations and Commercial Marketing teams as we establish and scale our manufacturing facility at our Indianapolis headquarters. This is a high-impact role for a commercially minded finance professional who enjoys working close to the business, influencing decisions, and driving operational and financial performance. You will act as a trusted advisor to Operations leaders, supporting capital investment, cost management, and operational efficiency, while also partnering with Commercial Marketing alongside the Commercial Finance Business Partner. The role includes line management of one direct report and offers significant exposure to senior leadership during a critical phase of growth.
· Partner closely with Operations leadership to support the set-up and ongoing performance of our manufacturing facility
· Lead financial planning activities including budgets, forecasts, and capital expenditure planning
· Support with building and evaluating business cases, investment proposals, ROI assessments and scenario analyses to support strategic decision-making
· Drive cost management and value optimization initiatives across relevant spend
· Support long-term planning and performance analysis 
· Develop clear, actionable financial reporting and performance insights for senior stakeholders
· Track key financial and operational KPIs, identifying risks and opportunities
· Continuously improving financial processes, controls, and reporting as operations scale
· Manage, coach, and develop one finance team member
Driven by high performance
What do you need to bring to the team?
· Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field
· CPA, CMA, or similar professional qualification preferred
· Proven experience of progressive finance experience, including experience supporting Operations or Manufacturing
· Strong financial modeling, analytical, and problem-solving skills
· Proven ability to influence and partner with senior stakeholders in a fast-paced environment
· Experience with budgeting, forecasting, capital investment, and cost management
· Track record of driving cost optimization or operational efficiency initiatives
· Comfortable working autonomously while collaborating across functions
· Experience using business intelligence tools such as Microsoft Power BI
Please note: This role is subject to U.S. export control restrictions. Successful candidates must either be a U.S. Person (defined by U.S. law as U.S. citizen, U.S. permanent resident, asylee, or refugee) or a non-U.S. Person who is eligible to obtain any required export control authorization
A team like no other.
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team challenges conventions and redefines success through bold ambition, cutting-edge innovation, and an unwavering commitment to precision and excellence—on and off the track. This includes offering medical, generous time off and, as part of a global brand, huge potential for career development. 

As an equal opportunities employer, we are committed to the equal treatment of all current and prospective employees and does not condone discrimination on the basis of age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, race or ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity or marriage and civil partnership. We aspire to have a diverse and inclusive workplace and strongly encourage suitably qualified applicants from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.
At The Cadillac Formula 1® Team, all Team Members are expected to actively support and uphold our policies and procedures, including those focused on Environmental responsibility, Sustainability initiatives, Inclusion and Health and Safety practices.
Please note that additional security checks may be required as part of the recruitment process. This may include a background check covering a minimum of the past five years and a criminal record check.

The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is The American Original—bringing a bold, American-led vision to the future of Formula 1™. Set to join the grid in 2026, the team is built from the ground up and designed for the global stage—driven by innovation, shaped for culture and engineered to deliver long-term brand affinity.

The Cadillac Formula 1® Team reflects the ambition, optimism and ingenuity that define the American spirit—balanced with global sensibility and deep respect for the sport’s legacy.

AND Digital is on a mission to close the world’s digital skills gap. We do that by doing things differently when we work with our clients, communities and colleagues. title: Senior Marketing Executive, App Marketing
description_text:
Senior App Marketing Executive (up to £40,000 depending on experience)
At Immediate, we’re home to some of the biggest and most loved consumer brands in the UK, including Radio Times, Good Food, Gardeners’ World and History Extra. Our trusted, quality content reaches millions of people a month across digital, print, video, podcasts, apps and live events. 
We’re the destination for people looking to get more from the things they love. We help inspire them; deepening their knowledge, skills and fuelling their passions, to enjoy more of what life has to offer. 
Whether it’s the big things, or the little things; from reaching fitness goals to savouring good food, cultivating outdoor, or indoor, spaces, or enjoying the latest TV sensation with loved ones – we help bring our audiences joy. 
About the role 
The Senior App Marketing Exec plays a key role within Immediate’s Customer Marketing & Subscriptions team, driving growth and performance across our app portfolio, with a focus on our Food and Entertainment brands. A strategic and commercially minded marketer, you’ll bring expertise in app ecosystems, acquisition, and subscription models to help accelerate the next stage of our app growth journey. 
You'll deliver Immediate's App Store optimisation strategy and deliver continuous improvement across acquisition, conversion, and monetisation. Working closely with Product, Tech, Brand, and Data teams, you'll develop and deliver campaigns and initiatives that enhance app visibility, optimise spend and deliver measurable growth 
This cross-functional role sits at the centre of Immediate digital transformation - blending creativity, analytics, and commercial focus to ensure our apps play a vital role in achieving our ambitious subscription goals. 
As a Senior App Marketing Executive, you will;
Manage and grow app store marketing strategy across multiple iOS and Android apps across Immediate brands. 
Support the Acquisition team in planning and delivering app marketing campaigns to drive digital subscription growth. 
Brief and collaborate with our design team to create multi-channel campaign assets, maintaining brand consistency and tone of voice. 
Manage App Store Optimisation (ASO) across keyword strategy, metadata testing, in-app event strategy, feature nomination and creative optimisation (icons, screenshots, video assets). 
Design and run structured experimentation programmes across ASO, paid acquisition, and in-app journeys, using insight to continually improve visibility and conversion in app stores. 
Use AppsFlyer (MMP) and in-app analytics tools to track, attribute, and optimise performance across acquisition and lifecycle journeys. 
Help shape clear, actionable dashboards and reports that turn data into decisions and identify opportunities for incremental growth. 
Grow subscriber LTV and further optimise Apple App Store/Google PlayStore CACs leveraging ASO and app store ads. 
Work cross-functionally with Product, Tech, Brand, and CRM teams to align growth initiatives with our broader digital subscription strategy. 
Build strong partnerships with Apple, AppsFlyer and other 3rd parties to stay close to platform developments and new opportunities 
Act as Immediate’s in-house app marketing expert – sharing knowledge, best practice, and insight across teams 
Proven experience in digital marketing campaigns and app marketing, within a consumer subscription or digital media business. 
Deep understanding of ASO, mobile performance marketing, and in-app monetisation. 
Expertise in AppsFlyer (or another MMP) with a strong grasp of tracking, attribution, and deep linking. 
Strong analytical and reporting skills, able to translate complex data into clear, actionable insight. 
Experience with paid acquisition across Apple Search Ads, Google App Campaigns, and Meta. 
Experience operating in a post-IDFA environment, with practical knowledge of SKAN and privacy-first measurement. 
You’ll bring ideas to life, ensuring every campaign is delivered on time and to a high standard - comfortable experimenting, iterating, and learning fast. 
Strong organisational and communication skills with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks and stakeholders simultaneously. 
A relaxed working environment with regular socials including a summer festival
Supportive well-being initiatives and benefits, talks & workshops, and Mental Health First aiders & Champions
25 days holiday plus a day for your birthday. Our offices will be closed between Christmas and New Year’s which are in addition to your annual entitlement
Tailored training and development through both our inhouse learning platform and LinkedIn Learning
A progressive and transparent culture focused on your development
Flexible / hybrid working plus early finish Fridays
Cycle to work scheme
Enhanced Family Policies including paternity, adoption and surrogacy leave. We also provide a pregnancy loss, fertility, and carers policy
Competitive pension plans and Life Assurance
A newly renovated modern office with lots of collaborative spaces
At Immediate, we pride ourselves on our open, collaborative, and dynamic culture that empowers our people to thrive and give their best. We care deeply about our people, environment, and impact. Our active Diversity & Inclusion network, comprehensive well-being programs, and clear sustainability strategies reflect this commitment.
People are at the heart of our business and creating a diverse and inclusive working environment is extremely important to us. Immediate is an equal opportunities employer. We’ll never treat anyone less favourably because of their sex, gender reassignment, pregnancy and maternity, marital/civil partnerships, sexual orientation, race, nationality, ethnic origin, age, religion or belief or disability. We’re also committed to supporting applications from those who are returning to work following a career break, maternity leave or caring responsibilities.
Immediate is a place where you can grow, be supported, and make a difference

title: Chargé de trésorerie (H/F/X) - Lyon
description_text:
Lieu : Lyon
Date souhaitée de démarrage : 01/03/2026 : ASAP 
Durée : CDD 18 mois 
Handicap International / Humanité & Inclusion (HI) est une association de solidarité internationale indépendante et impartiale, qui intervient dans les situations de pauvreté et d’exclusion, de conflits et de catastrophes. Œuvrant aux côtés des personnes handicapées et vulnérabilisées, elle agit et témoigne pour répondre à leurs besoins essentiels et améliorer leurs conditions de vie. Elle s’engage à promouvoir le respect de leur dignité et de leurs droits fondamentaux. Depuis sa création en 1982, HI a mis en place des programmes de développement dans plus de 60 pays et intervient dans de nombreuses situations d’urgence avec ses 4000 collaborateurs actuellement. 
Nous croyons fermement en l'importance de l'inclusion et de la diversité au sein de notre structure. C'est pourquoi nous sommes engagés dans une politique handicap afin de favoriser l'accueil et l'intégration de personnes en situation de handicap. 
Merci d’indiquer si vous avez besoin d’un aménagement particulier, y compris pour participer aux 1ers entretiens.
Retrouvez plus d’informations sur l’association : www.hi.org.
OBJECTIFS DU POSTE :
Au sein d’une équipe de 6 personnes, vous effectuez les paiements bancaires, suivez les versements reçus et contribuez à la clôture comptable, au respect des standards de l’organisation ainsi qu’au cadre réglementaire du pays. Votre rôle permet d’assurer la liquidité quotidienne de l’organisation et de maîtriser les risques de trésorerie.
Afin d’atteindre cet objectif, vous : 
Mission 1 : Responsable des paiements
• Après contrôle des informations bancaires et de la cohérence de la demande par rapport aux besoins, vous réalisez les opérations de transfert d’argent
• Vous transmettez les ordres de virement aux Signataires de Trésorerie et mettez à disposition la documentation afférente.
• Vous vérifiez la bonne affectation des paiements bancaires automatisés dans le Système d’Information Trésorerie.
• Vous instruisez les anomalies de paiement et les demandes d’information des banques jusqu’à pouvoir effectuer le paiement ou le transfert de fonds.
• Vous préparez les chèques et la documentation afférente et les soumettez aux Trésoriers siège.
• Vous créez, mettez à jour et contrôlez les coordonnées bancaires des tiers dans le SIT.
Mission 2 : Vous tenez la comptabilité et participez aux travaux de clôture liés à la trésorerie
• Vous saisissez quotidiennement les opérations d’encaissements et de décaissements dans le SI comptable.
• Vous effectuez le rapprochement entre la base de données donateur et les données du Système d’Information Comptable (SIC) et, en cas d’écart, vous travaillez avec le service donateur pour identifier l’erreur, la corriger ou la justifier.
• Vous mettez quotidiennement à disposition les avis de versement aux différents services.
• Vous mettez à jour et à disposition des Contrôleurs de Gestion des Financements (CGF) le tableau des versements non identifiés et vous les relancez si les informations ne sont pas communiquées.
• Vous effectuez mensuellement le rapprochement des soldes des comptes bancaires avec les soldes des journaux dans le SIC et, en cas d’écarts, vous identifiez l’origine pour les justifier.
• Vous réévaluez les soldes des comptes bancaires et des caisses en devises.
• Vous contrôlez les comptes d’attente et relancez les demandes de pièces justificatives pour pouvoir les épurer.
• Vous transmettez les justificatifs comptables et de trésorerie aux auditeurs.
Mission 3 : Gestion de la trésorerie
• Vous établissez quotidiennement la position de la trésorerie sur votre périmètre, mettez à jour le prévisionnel et alertez le/la Trésorier(ière) Siège sur les besoins de liquidité.
• Vous contribuez aux différents chantiers qui impactent votre service et à l’évolution des outils et procédures de trésorerie.
• Vous êtes responsable du traitement des mails reçus sur les boîtes mail génériques.
• Vous participez à la formation de vos pairs.
• Vous sollicitez les établissements bancaires ou les autres services pour obtenir les documents justificatifs.
• Vous pouvez être amené(e) à assurer temporairement le remplacement d’un(e) autre Chargé(e) de Trésorerie.
• Vous êtes le premier interlocuteur des Contrôleurs Financiers sur les questions de trésorerie.
Mission 4 : Participation à l’animation du métier
Expérience professionnelle de 4 ans minimum au sein d’un pôle trésorerie dans un environnement international, en tant que chargé de trésorerie expérimenté.
Une expérience en ONG est un atout majeur.
Maîtrise des logiciels bureautiques courants, avec un niveau avancé sur Excel.
Connaissance du S.I. Navision est un plus.
Maîtrise des logiciels de gestion de trésorerie (type Sage, Reuters, Kyriba).
Bonnes connaissances en gestion de trésorerie et fonctionnement du réseau bancaire international.
Compréhension des données comptables et de la gestion comptable des opérations bancaires et de trésorerie.
Méthode et rigueur sont des qualités indispensables à votre profil
Une aisance relationnelle et une capacité à travailler en équipe sont également requis
Vous savez communiquer en langue française et anglaise à l’écrit comme à l’oral. 
CONDITIONS DU POSTE :
CDI à partir du 1er février 2026
Ce que vous trouverez chez nous :
· Des valeurs fortes : Humanité, Inclusion, Engagement et intégrité ! Et une proximité avec le terrain via des conférences régulières.
· Statut cadre
· 25 jours de congés payés et 22,5 jours de RTT annuel permettant de concilier un équilibre vie pro/ vie perso
· Une organisation flexible : carte tickets resto (prise en charge 60% par HI), charte de télétravail : 8 jours de présentiel par mois minimum 
· Transport : un forfait mobilité durable existe. Prise en charge 50% des abonnements transports. 
· Bien-être et santé : mutuelle et prévoyance, sensibilisations internes diverses, un pôle Qualité de Vie et Conditions de Travail actif
· CSE et avantages sociaux : Œuvres sociales du CSE pour améliorer votre quotidien.
· Une vie associative culturelle, sportive et sociale dynamique
ACCESSIBILITE DU LIEU DE TRAVAIL : 
Les locaux sont facilement accessibles en transports en commun (bus, métro). Un parking voitures et un parc à vélos sont également à disposition. Au sein du bâtiment, des rampes d'accès et ascenseurs garantissent une meilleure circulation. Tous les postes de travail sont situés en Open Space mais des box sont disponibles à chaque étage pour travailler dans le calme si nécessaire. L’espace de travail est très lumineux. 
Une référente handicap est présente pour répondre aux éventuelles questions et vous accompagner dans vos démarches. En fonction de vos besoins, le poste peut être aménagé.
Les candidatures sont traitées de façon continue, n’attendez pas pour postuler ! 
Seules les candidatures retenues seront contactées

title: Disaster Recovery Test Coordinator
description_text:
D-ploy is an IT and Engineering Solutions company with operations throughout the EMEA region including Switzerland, Germany, Czech Republic, Austria, UK, as well as the USA. 
We pride ourselves on delivering innovative and superior services and solutions to numerous industry-leading clients. By building relationships and trusted partnerships within the IT community, we optimize our customer‘s IT productivity and contribute to the organization’s success and value.
We are interested in talking to engaging, flexible, and solution-oriented individuals who are looking to become a part of a dynamically growing and international organization. We are focused on creating value where IT counts, join us!
Tasks and Responsibilities
As a Disaster Recovery Test Coordinator, you will be responsible for overseeing and coordinating all aspects of disaster recovery testing within an organization. Your main objective will be to ensure that the organization's disaster recovery plans are effective and can be executed seamlessly in the event of a disaster or disruptive event. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to plan, coordinate, and execute disaster recovery tests, ensuring that all systems and processes are thoroughly tested and validated and to coordinate preparation of DR Tests Reports and outputs.
Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with various teams including IT, operations, security, and business units to identify critical systems, applications, and data that need to be included in the disaster recovery testing.
• Coordinate and facilitate regular disaster recovery testing exercises to evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of the organization's disaster recovery plans.
• Define and document test scenarios, scripts, and procedures to be followed during disaster recovery tests.
• Work closely with DR Solution Architects and other IT technical teams to ensure that all necessary infrastructure, systems, and applications are set up and ready for testing.
• Coordinate with external vendors or third-party service providers to ensure their involvement and adherence to disaster recovery testing plans.
• Monitor and track the progress of disaster recovery tests, identifying and resolving any issues or obstacles that may arise.
• Document and report test results, including any gaps or areas for improvement in the disaster recovery plans.
• Conduct post-test analysis and evaluations to identify lessons learned and recommendations for enhancing the organization's disaster recovery capabilities.
• Stay updated with the latest industry best practices, trends, and technologies related to disaster recovery testing.
• Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, or a related field.
• Proven experience (2 years) in disaster recovery planning, testing, and coordination.
• Strong understanding of disaster recovery concepts, methodologies, and industry best practices.
• In-depth knowledge of relevant technologies, systems, and tools used in disaster recovery testing like SNOW/BCM is a plus
• Excellent project management and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively manage multiple tasks and priorities.
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate and build relationships with cross-functional teams.
• Analytical mindset with the ability to identify, troubleshoot, and resolve issues during disaster recovery testing
• Certification in disaster recovery planning (e.g., Certified Business Continuity Professional (CBCP), Disaster Recovery Certified Professional (DRCP)) is a plus.
Broad range of activities, tasks, and projects
Flexible working conditions
Vouchers (B-day voucher, wedding, and new born surprise)
Fishing for Friends program – our referral program
Refreshments in the D-ploy office
Further development and professional advancement 
Friendly and international working environment 
Company-sponsored events
Competitive salary and various benefits
Is IT in your DNA?

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in a related field and at least 2 years of experience in disaster recovery planning and testing. Strong understanding of disaster recovery concepts and excellent project management skills are essential.

linkedin_org_description:
D-ploy is an IT Engineering and Consulting Company with a wide portfolio of services and solutions suitable for globally as well as regionally operating companies. 
Our strength is in providing proven standard and individually tailored solutions helping our customers achieve their strategic and business goals. 
Our core competencies are operational IT Infrastructure services and consulting in the areas of Service Management, Program and Project Management as well as Quality Management. 

Company Profile
Founded in January 2003 in Kaiseraugst, Switzerland, D-ploy has been growing steadily by expanding its expertise, portfolio and geographical presence across Europe and in the US. Our main focus has always been on ensuring that IT enables the Business, supports value generation and contributes to the overall work efficiency. With the customer-centric attitude and pragmatic approach to IT, we became a trusted and preferred IT partner to many customers proving again and again, that we can do IT.
Building our expertise and establishing long-term partnerships with our customers, we understand the value of sustainable practices driven by compliance with industry standards and implementing best practices. Therefore, since 2016 D-ploy has been ISO 9001 and ISO/IEC 20000-1 and as of 2017 ISO/IEC 27001 certified. 
We strive to continuously offer the most suitable and innovative service solutions to our current and future customers by staying agile, understanding the industry needs and keeping up to date on the market trends.

title: Product Owner
description_text:
We are looking for a Product Owner who is eager to learn, take ownership, and help shape how our teams build products that make a real difference.
At Stadium, we’re building products that bring delight and connection to organizations worldwide. From thoughtful gifting to brand-building experiences, our platform combines creativity, data, and technology to make every interaction meaningful.
In this role, you will work closely with a Senior Product Owner to translate business goals and user insights into clear, well-defined product features. You will collaborate with teams across Engineering, Design, Operations, Customer Success, and Marketing to design scalable, impactful solutions.
This role is ideal for someone with experience in a startup or product-led environment who wants to grow in a high-ownership, fast-moving, and collaborative setting.
Your Role
You will be responsible for:
Collaborating with Product and Design teams to define product vision, priorities, and goals for specific modules or initiatives. 
Maintaining and refining the product backlog with clear, actionable, and business-aligned user stories. 
Supporting discovery, sprint planning, and delivery activities to ensure consistent execution. 
Partnering with Operations, Customer Success, Engineering, Marketing, and Design teams to understand workflows, challenges, and SOPs. 
Identifying inefficiencies and opportunities for automation or product improvements. 
Translating cross-functional insights into structured requirements and user stories. 
Working with engineers and designers to clarify requirements and ensure successful implementation. 
Supporting Project Managers in aligning priorities, dependencies, and timelines. 
Using analytics and feedback to measure product success and propose iterative enhancements. 
Gradually taking ownership of smaller product areas with mentorship and guidance. 
Staying curious about user needs, team workflows, and emerging product trends. 
What You'll Bring to the Table
Primary Requirements:
3+ years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or in a similar role within a startup or product-led tech environment. 
Curious, analytical mindset with a strong desire to learn from senior product leaders while taking initiative. 
Strong problem-solving and prioritization skills. 
Excellent communication and stakeholder collaboration abilities. 
Ability to understand both customer experience and internal operational workflows. 
Familiarity with Agile methodologies (Scrum and/or Kanban). 
Hands-on experience with tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Figma, or analytics dashboards.
Competitive salary based on skill set.
Fully remote role - work with talented teammates across the globe. 
Flexible working hours and autonomy over your schedule. 
5-day work week for better work-life balance.
Team retreats and offsites to connect and recharge. 
Meal vouchers and employee benefits(health insurance) support. 
Internal learning tracks to grow into other areas of development. 
Plenty of company swag and additional perks.

title: Founding AI Engineer
description_text:
Founding AI Engineer
Location: San Francisco, CA (In-person)
Compensation: $150K – $200K • 0.10% – 0.25% Equity
We believe that the way engineering output is measured today is fundamentally broken. Our mission is to build the definitive platform for understanding and improving how engineering teams work. By leveraging modern AI, we provide teams with a far more accurate and actionable view of productivity, impact, and collaboration—moving beyond vanity metrics and surveillance toward better engineering outcomes.
As our Founding AI Engineer, you will be responsible for building the intelligence that understands the nuances of software engineering work. You aren't just implementing models; you are building the processes and standards that will define our technical foundation. You will work directly with our CTO (MIT alum, founding engineer background) and CEO (experienced sales leader) in a high-intensity, $4.2M seed-funded environment.
Key Responsibilities:
Architect AI Systems: Build and deploy AI models that analyze how engineers work, surfacing insights into quality and collaboration.
Define Standards: Create the internal processes that make building incremental features and models faster and more reliable.
Product Innovation: Partner with founders to rethink engineering output from first principles.
Customer Delight: Deliver "10x" intelligence features that make the lives of engineering leaders and their teams significantly easier.
Formidable Engineering Mindset: You are a relentless problem solver who stops at nothing to accomplish a goal.
The "High Slope" Trait: You are a very good engineer committed to becoming a great one. We value your rate of improvement over your current static skill set.
Pragmatism: You know how to operate in a startup environment where you must balance "extinguishing fires" with long-term building.
Technical Proficiency: 1+ years of experience with Python and Go. You must be comfortable shipping production-ready systems.
High Empathy & Communication: You care deeply about how users think and can communicate technical complexity clearly to both engineers and executives.
Grit: You are accustomed to picking the hard option and pushing through it.
Eligibility: Must be a US Citizen or current Visa holder.
Nice-to-Haves:
Experience with React and TypeScript.
Previous deep thought or work regarding engineering productivity.
Passable design sensibilities.
Ground Floor Opportunity: Join a YC-backed startup (W25) during its most formative stage.
Direct Ownership: High equity stakes and the chance to shape a product used by millions of engineers.
Mentorship: Collaborative environment with founders who have scaled teams from $0 to $30M in ARR.
Due to the high volume of applications we anticipate, we regret that we are unable to provide individual feedback to all candidates. If you do not hear back from us within 4 weeks of your application, please assume that you have not been successful on this occasion. We genuinely appreciate your interest and wish you the best in your job search.
Commitment to Equality and Accessibility:
At MLabs, we are committed to offer equal opportunities to all candidates. We ensure no discrimination, accessible job adverts, and providing information in accessible formats. Our goal is to foster a diverse, inclusive workplace with equal opportunities for all. If you need any reasonable adjustments during any part of the hiring process or you would like to see the job-advert in an accessible format please let us know at the earliest opportunity by emailing human-resources@mlabs.city.
MLabs Ltd collects and processes the personal information you provide such as your contact details, work history, resume, and other relevant data for recruitment purposes only. This information is managed securely in accordance with MLabs Ltd’s Privacy Policy and Information Security Policy, and in compliance with applicable data protection laws. Your data may be shared only with clients and trusted partners where necessary for recruitment purposes. You may request the deletion of your data or withdraw your consent at any time by contacting legal@mlabs.city.

This role involves partnering with founders to drive product innovation and deliver highly impactful intelligence features for engineering teams.

A relentless problem-solving mindset, grit, and the ability to operate pragmatically in a startup environment are essential traits.

title: GP/Transfusion Medicine-Government Healthcare Facility
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is pleased to announce an exciting career opportunity for a GP/Transfusion Medicine Specialist at a respected government healthcare facility. This position merges the responsibilities of general practice with specialized transfusion medicine, offering a chance to contribute to public health and patient well-being.
As a GP/Transfusion Medicine Specialist, you will provide essential primary care while ensuring the safe and effective management of blood transfusions. Your role will involve close collaboration with healthcare professionals to establish best practices in transfusion medicine and educate patients and staff about transfusion-related protocols.

Responsibilities
Deliver primary care services, assessing and treating a wide range of medical conditions.
Evaluate patient transfusion needs, prescribing appropriate blood products based on clinical judgment.
Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to develop and implement transfusion policies and procedures.
Educate patients on transfusion processes, risks, and benefits, ensuring informed consent is obtained.
Monitor patients for adverse reactions during and after transfusions and manage any complications effectively.
Participate in quality assurance and improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing transfusion practices.
Stay updated on advancements in transfusion medicine and general healthcare practices.
Medical degree with a valid license to practice as a General Practitioner.
Additional training or specialization in Transfusion Medicine is highly preferred.
Experience in clinical and transfusion settings, particularly within government healthcare frameworks, is advantageous.
Strong commitment to public health and patient safety standards.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for effective patient and team interactions.
Analytical skills to assess clinical data and respond to patient needs efficiently.
A collaborative approach to working in a multidisciplinary healthcare environment.
Willingness to engage in continuous professional development and stay informed about industry trends.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus

Responsibilities also include educating patients about transfusion processes and monitoring for adverse reactions.

title: Senior Pipeline Engineer
locations_alt_raw: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
locations_derived: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
description_text:
We are? 

We are EnerMech, a specialist service company that delivers safe, integrated solutions for complex energy projects. For over 50 years, we’ve been energizing our clients across the world with unrivaled expertise, equipment, and technology that supports the entire asset life cycle – offshore and on land. We enable progress through our three global business lines: Energy Solutions, Infrastructure & Industrial Solutions, and Lifting Solutions. Within these areas, we offer a wide range of services, including cranes, lifting, fluid power, training, equipment rental, pipeline, subsea, process, and valves – all designed to help you optimize performance, enhance reliability, and improve efficiency. With a focus on operational excellence, we deliver value through our customized integrated solutions. This approach reduces risk and enhances efficiencies making us a leading and trusted partner every step of the way.

Diversity & Inclusion
EnerMech is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive organization. We believe that diversity enriches our team, and we welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, cultures, and identities.
The Role? 

As a Senior Pipeline Engineer at EnerMech, you will lead the design, analysis, and delivery of pipeline projects from inception through to completion, ensuring safety, quality, and compliance with industry standards. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in pipeline engineering, project management, and a thorough understanding of materials and methods used in pipeline construction and rehabilitation.
Main Responsibilities & Key Result Areas
Lead and manage pipeline engineering projects, ensuring alignment with client specifications and regulatory requirements.
Perform detailed design and engineering calculations for pipeline systems.
Evaluate and select materials for pipeline construction, ensuring adherence to industry standards.
Coordinate with cross-functional teams, including project managers, inspectors, and construction crews.
Conduct risk assessments and develop mitigation strategies for potential engineering challenges.
Supervise and mentor junior engineers and technical staff.
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of industry trends, technologies, and best practices in pipeline engineering.
10+ years of relevant experience in pipeline engineering, with a strong portfolio of successful project deliveries.
Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Civil, or Petroleum Engineering (or equivalent).
Proficient in pipeline design software and engineering tools (e.g., AutoCAD, CAESAR II).
Strong understanding of pipeline integrity management and maintenance practices.
Ability to interpret codes and standards, such as ASME, API, and DNV.
Excellent communication, problem-solving, and leadership skills.
Behaviors and Personal Qualities
Proactive and results-driven mindset, with a strong commitment to quality and safety.
Ability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team environment.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines.
Willingness to travel to project sites as required.

title: Senior OT Architect
description_text:
Sword is a leading provider of business technology solutions within the Energy, Public and Finance Sectors, driving transformational change within our clients. We use proven technology, specialist teams and domain expertise to build solid technical foundations across platforms, data, and business applications. We have a passion for using technology to solve business problems, working in partnership with our clients to help in achieving their goals. 
About the role:
As a Senior OT Architect, you will play a leading role in shaping how organisations design, evolve and secure their operational technology environments. This is a senior, client-facing position focused on discovery, architectural design and technical leadership across complex OT landscapes. You will work closely with senior stakeholders, delivery teams and engineering specialists to define practical, scalable solutions that reflect the realities of operational environments.
The role sits at the intersection of strategy and delivery — guiding architectural direction, supporting major programmes of work, and ensuring that designs are grounded, implementable and aligned to operational needs. You will act as a trusted technical authority across engagements, helping organisations understand their current environments, define improvement roadmaps and establish the foundations required to support long-term resilience and maturity.
Working within our IACS Community:
Our Industrial Automation & Control Systems (IACS) capability sits at the heart of how we support organisations operating complex OT and ICS environments. As part of this practice, you’ll be part of a community of experienced architects, consultants and engineers who collaborate across a wide range of operational settings. Knowledge gained from live environments is shared openly, helping shape how the practice continues to evolve.
Architects operate with a high degree of autonomy while remaining closely connected to the wider team — providing technical leadership, supporting delivery teams, and contributing to the development of standards, methodologies and architectural approaches across the practice. This is an environment where experience is valued, collaboration is natural, and individuals play a meaningful role in shaping how OT services are delivered.
As a Senior OT Architect, you will:
Lead discovery engagements and define architectural approaches across complex OT environments 
Design end-to-end OT solutions aligned to operational requirements, safety considerations and long-term sustainability 
Partner with commercial and delivery teams to support technical proposals, scope definition and solution shaping 
Act as the senior technical escalation point across OT engagements 
Lead and oversee site surveys to understand OT systems, networks and asset landscapes 
Guide security and compliance assessments across operational environments 
Facilitate risk identification and support the development of mitigation strategies 
Develop architectural roadmaps to support capability uplift and long-term improvement 
Support the creation of governance models, policies, standards and operating frameworks 
Oversee third-party suppliers and technical partners across project delivery 
Present architectural approaches, findings and recommendations to senior stakeholders 
Provide technical leadership, mentoring and direction to OT consultants and engineers 
Work alongside project managers to ensure delivery aligns with scope, timelines and outcomes 
Significant experience working within OT, ICS or industrial environments in an architectural, senior consultant or technical leadership capacity 
Strong background in one or more OT domains, such as: 
Network architecture and segmentation 
Asset discovery and environment visibility 
Governance, policy and standards 
Endpoint protection in operational environments 
Risk assessment and situational review 
Experience designing and shaping OT solutions across complex environments 
Proven ability to develop roadmaps and structured improvement approaches 
Experience producing detailed technical reports and architectural deliverables 
Confidence presenting complex technical concepts to senior and executive stakeholders 
Strong understanding of cyber security processes, tools and implementation approaches within OT contexts 
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills 
Desirable
Significant experience within OT security, industrial environments or critical infrastructure 
Previous consultancy experience across sectors such as energy, oil & gas, chemicals, manufacturing or utilities 
Knowledge of recognised industry frameworks and standards including: 
IEC 62443 
NERC CIP 
NESA 
OG86 
GICSP or equivalent certifications 
Additional cyber security certifications such as CISSP, GCIA, GCIH, CEH
At Sword, our core values and culture are based on caring about our people, investing in training and career development, and building inclusive teams where we are all encouraged to contribute to achieve success. We offer comprehensive benefits designed to support your professional development and enhance your overall quality of life. In addition to a Competitive Salary, here's what you can expect as part of our benefits package:
Personalised Career Development: We create a development plan customised to your goals and aspirations, with a range of learning and development opportunities within a culture that encourages growth.
Flexible working: Flexible work arrangements to support your work-life balance. We can’t promise to always be able to meet every request, however, are keen to discuss your individual preferences to make it work where we can.
A Fantastic Benefits Package: This includes generous annual leave allowance, enhanced family friendly benefits, pension scheme, access to private health, well-being, and insurance schemes.
At Sword we are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and are proud to be an equal opportunities employer, ensuring that all applicants receive fair and equal consideration for employment, regardless of whether they meet every requirement. If you don’t tick all the boxes but feel you have some of the relevant skills and experience we’re looking for, please do consider applying and highlight your transferable skills and experience. We embrace diversity in all its forms, valuing individuals regardless of age, disability, gender identity or reassignment, marital or civil partner status, pregnancy or maternity status, race, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, sex, or sexual orientation. Your perspective and potential are important to us.

title: PPU Technician-N2/FM/Machining
description_text:
About EnerMech:
EnerMech is a global leader in providing specialist engineering services to the energy, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With over 50 years of experience, we consistently deliver tailored solutions that enhance performance, reliability, and efficiency for our clients around the world.
Position Overview:
The PPU Technician (N2/FM/Machining) will be responsible for the setup, operation, and maintenance of various power pack units (PPU) along with capabilities in nitrogen (N2) applications, flow management (FM), and machining processes. This role is essential for ensuring the efficient performance of our machinery and equipment during critical operations.
Key Responsibilities:
Operate and maintain power pack units to ensure optimal performance.
Carry out nitrogen gas operations and maintenance activities.
Perform machining processes including setup, operation, and maintenance of relevant machinery.
Conduct troubleshooting and repairs on PPU and associated systems.
Ensure compliance with safety standards and operational procedures.
Document maintenance activities, inspections, and any relevant findings accurately.
Collaborate effectively with other technicians and teams to ensure seamless operations.
Qualifications and Skills:
Relevant trade qualification or experience in power pack units, nitrogen systems, and machining.
Proven experience in operating and maintaining machinery within industrial settings.
Strong understanding of hydraulic and pneumatic systems.
Ability to read and interpret technical manuals and diagrams.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork capabilities.
Commitment to adhere to safety and quality standards.

title: Intensive care-Government Healthcare Facility
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is excited to partner with a reputable Government Healthcare Facility that is committed to delivering high-quality medical care to the community. We are seeking a highly skilled and compassionate Intensive Care Specialist to join their Intensive Care Unit (ICU) team. This role offers a unique opportunity to make a meaningful difference in patients' lives while working in a supportive and dynamic environment.
As an Intensive Care Specialist at this Government Healthcare Facility, you will be responsible for providing advanced medical care to critically ill patients. You will work alongside a dedicated team of healthcare professionals to assess, diagnose, and implement treatment plans tailored to each patient's needs. Your expertise and collaborative approach will ensure the highest standards of patient care and safety.
Key Responsibilities
Conduct thorough evaluations and assessments of critically ill patients in the ICU.
Develop, implement, and monitor individualized treatment plans in collaboration with the healthcare team.
Administer medications, therapies, and medical interventions as necessary.
Monitor patients' progress and vital signs, making adjustments to treatment as required.
Provide effective communication and support to patients' families, addressing their concerns and providing updates.
Maintain accurate and detailed documentation of patient care activities and outcomes.
Participate in quality improvement initiatives and continuing education to enhance patient care practices.
Medical degree with specialization in Intensive Care Medicine or related field.
Current medical license to practice in the region, with board certification in an appropriate specialty.
Proven experience working in an intensive care environment, with a strong understanding of critical care protocols.
Excellent clinical skills and the ability to respond effectively to emergencies.
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills for interacting with patients, families, and colleagues.
Detail-oriented and organized, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Commitment to ethical medical practices and patient advocacy.
Benefits:
- 30 Days Annual Leave (Paid).
- Annual Air Fare 
- Medical Insurance for self & family as per the UAE Labor Law.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Intensive Care Specialist will conduct evaluations and assessments of critically ill patients, develop and implement treatment plans, and monitor patient progress. They will also communicate effectively with patients' families and participate in quality improvement initiatives.

title: Offshore Crane Supervisor
description_text:
We are? 

A leading specialist engineering services company delivering integrated solutions for complex energy and infrastructure projects and operating assets. Our business is built on the belief that our people are what make EnerMech excellent. When we are thriving globally it’s not just about investing in our infrastructure, service offering and technology, but by also investing in our people.
The Role
The Offshore Crane Supervisor will be responsible for managing and overseeing crane operations on offshore platforms, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational efficiencies. This role requires strong leadership skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving in critical situations.
Main Responsibilities & Key Result Areas
Oversee all lift planning and execution procedures to maintain safety and operational integrity. 
Ensure that lifting operations are conducted in compliance with industry standards and company policies.
Supervise crane operators and riggers to ensure proper execution of lifting operations and adherence to defined procedures.
Review and approve lifting plans, conducting risk assessments and ensuring all necessary permits are acquired prior to operations.
Conduct regular inspections of cranes and lifting equipment, identifying any maintenance needs or safety concerns.
Coordinate with project management to report on crane operation progress, issues, and equipment performance.
Lead daily safety meetings with the team to address hazards and promote a culture of safety.
Train and mentor junior personnel in crane operations and safety practices.
Maintain thorough records of all crane operations, maintenance, and incidents.
Engage with clients to provide updates and receive feedback on lifting operations.
Essential: 
Proven experience (minimum 5 years) in an offshore supervisory role with strong knowledge of crane operations and lifting practices.
Relevant certifications in lifting operations and safety, including LEEA qualifications preferred.
Strong leadership and communication skills to effectively manage a team and coordinate with clients.
Ability to work under pressure and make critical decisions in high-stakes situations.
Behaviors and Personal Qualities
Self-driven with a commitment to operational excellence.
Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to promote teamwork.
High standards of safety and quality in all lift operations.
EnerMech is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive organisation. We believe that diversity enriches our team, and we welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, cultures, and identities.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Offshore Crane Supervisor is responsible for managing and overseeing all crane operations on offshore platforms, ensuring strict adherence to safety standards and operational efficiency. Key duties include overseeing lift planning and execution, supervising personnel, reviewing lifting plans, and conducting regular equipment inspections.
ai_requirements_summary: Essential requirements include a minimum of five years of proven experience in an offshore supervisory role with strong knowledge of crane operations and lifting practices. Candidates must possess relevant certifications, such as LEEA qualifications, along with strong leadership, communication skills, and the ability to make critical decisions under pressure.
